originalTitle: Savage House review – Claire Foy and Richard E Grant sell it hard in bewigged 18th-century caper
summary: The leads are the most watchable thing in this raucous period yarn about a grimy pair of status-obsessed nobles
content: Black-belt performances from Claire Foy and Richard E Grant put some vim and vigour into this haranguingly one-note and unidirectional period romp of the raucously bewigged and be-poxed 18th century. It’s written and directed by American film-maker Peter Glanz, who gives us candlelit interiors like a knockoff Barry Lyndon, and periodic deafening orchestral stabs with a touch of Amadeus as furious people in costume storm down corridors. But Grant and Foy are always there, selling it hard and there are one or two nice lines. They play Sir Chauncey and Lady Savage, who are living in a vast crumbling country estate: he’s a parvenu, an adventurer, a lover of the new Hanover dispensation who loathes Jacobites, but fundamentally a social alpinist who married for money and took his wife’s noble name. She was entranced by his roguish ways and she forgave him everything but is, however, having an affair with the footman, Halifax (Jack Farthing), while he is carrying on with the maid, Dorothy (Bel Powley). Richard McCabe and Vicki Pepperdine play two ghastly neighbours with dodgy teeth: the only people who will associate with them. Yet just when it seems that the Savages are socially beyond the pale, they are thrilled beyond measure at the Duke and Duchess of Devonshire inviting themselves for dinner as part of some sort of quasi-royal “progress”. The Savages’ social stock price rockets, they borrow ruinously to make the house worthy of their guests’ imminent arrival and the slide towards calamity begins. It’s all a bit strenuous but Foy and Grant are such class acts that they make this watchable. • Savage House screened at SXSW London and is out on 5 June in the UK and US.
chineseTitle: 《野蛮之家》评论——克莱尔·福伊和理查德·E·格兰特在 18 世纪戴着假发的胡闹剧中大力推销它
chineseSummary: 在这部关于一对肮脏的、痴迷于地位的贵族的喧闹的时代故事中，主角是最值得关注的事情
chineseContent: 克莱尔·福伊 (Claire Foy) 和理查德·E·格兰特 (Richard E Grant) 的黑带表演为这部充满喧闹、戴着假发、长满痘痘的 18 世纪的单音、单向的长篇大论的时代嬉戏增添了一些活力和活力。该片由美国电影制片人彼得·格兰茨编剧和导演，他为我们呈现了仿冒巴里·林登的烛光内饰，以及周期性震耳欲聋的管弦乐，带有阿玛迪斯的风格，穿着戏服的愤怒的人们冲过走廊。但格兰特和福伊总是在那里，卖力地推销，并且有一两句漂亮的台词。他们扮演昌西爵士和萨维奇夫人，他们住在一个巨大的摇摇欲坠的乡村庄园里：他是一个暴发户，一个冒险家，一个新汉诺威制度的爱好者，厌恶雅各布派，但本质上是一个社会登山家，为了金钱而结婚，并继承了妻子的高贵名字。她被他的无赖行为迷住了，她原谅了他的一切，但她却与男仆哈利法克斯（杰克·法辛饰）有染，而他却与女仆多萝西（贝尔·波利饰）有染。理查德·麦凯布和维姬·佩珀代因扮演两个长着不安全牙齿的可怕邻居：唯一会与他们交往的人。然而，就在野蛮人似乎在社交方面超出了正常范围时，他们对德文郡公爵和公爵夫人邀请自己共进晚餐感到无比兴奋，这是某种准皇室“进步”的一部分。野蛮人的社会股价飙升，他们大举借钱以使房子适合即将到来的客人，灾难开始滑向。这一切都有点费劲，但福伊和格兰特的表演非常出色，让这部电影变得值得一看。 • Savage House 在伦敦 SXSW 放映，并于 6 月 5 日在英国和美国上映。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
